playlist.name: Season 1
canonical_episode_order: Semmelweis and the Rejection of Handwashing
Titanic and the Lifeboat Regulation Gap

composition_policy.requested_direction: only two production elements: large Season 1 title and Paper Architecture backplate with object aligned right
composition_policy.carrier: deterministic local title over approved title-free raster Paper Architecture source art
composition_policy.overlay_zone_policy: title never enters lower-right YouTube playlist overlay zone; object detail is de-emphasized there where possible

candidates.label: N - Minimal Right Anchor
candidates.summary: Large left Season 1 title over a darkened full-bleed kept backplate; shuttle/ship object held on the right.

candidates.label: O - Large Stacked
candidates.summary: Stacked Season/1 treatment for maximum small-size read; object remains on the right edge of the Paper Architecture backplate.

candidates.label: P - Wide Quiet Field
candidates.summary: Wider negative-space crop with Season 1 large on the left and the primary paper object held right of center.

candidates.label: Q - High Object
candidates.summary: Object sits high and right so the lower-right playlist overlay covers low-value backplate rather than the title.

candidates.label: R - Systems Backplate
candidates.summary: Broader channel-systems backplate with the main physical object pushed to the right and only Season 1 on the left.

candidates.label: S - First-Eight Backplate
candidates.summary: First-eight evidence-wall source used only as a text-free backplate, with the title occupying a clean left field.

candidates.label: T - Low Title
candidates.summary: Lower-left title placement for a quieter cover-card read; right object remains above the YouTube overlay zone.

candidates.label: U - RTL Mirror Ready
candidates.summary: RTL-ready LTR master: title zone and object zone are cleanly separable so the layout can be mirrored for Arabic/Hebrew localization.

rtl_scaling_recommendations: Use logical zones, not fixed left/right rules: text-start zone plus object-end zone.
For RTL localizations, create a mirrored sibling: localized title on the right/top-right, object/backplate anchor on the left, while keeping YouTube's lower-right overlay area text-free.
Keep source art text-free and line-free at the composition layer so Arabic/Hebrew title rendering can be swapped without regenerating the backplate.
Avoid arrows, progress bars, numbered episode strips, cause-chain rails, and left-to-right motion cues in season covers.
Use a generous title box because Arabic/Hebrew season phrasing can be wider/taller than English; validate at 320x180 and 168x94.
Prefer mirrored pairs over a single universal crop when the title is the primary read; prefer a centered object with upper-corner title only when one asset must serve every locale.
